data.caption: Figure 1: Visual illustration of the GraphSAGE sample and aggregate approach.

Figure 2: A: Timing experiments on Reddit data, with training batches of size 512 and inference on the full test set (79,534 nodes). B: Model performance with respect to the size of the sampled neighborhood, where the “neighborhood sample size” refers to the number of neighbors sampled at each depth for K = 2 with S1 = S2 (on the citation data using GraphSAGE-mean).
Figure 3: Accuracy (in F1-score) for different approaches on the citation data as the feature matrix is incrementally replaced with random Gaussian noise.
